quantification_methods: MS1 intensity based label-free quantification method

publications.ref: Barkovits K, Pacharra S, Pfeiffer K, Steinbach S, Eisenacher M, Marcus K, Uszkoreit J. Reproducibility, specificity and accuracy of relative quantification using spectral library-based data-independent acquisition. Mol Cell Proteomics. 2019

Characteristics[CellLine].evidence: No specific cell line mentioned in manuscript or anchor data

Characteristics[Treatment].evidence: gold standard spike-in sample set with known contents and ratios of proteins

Characteristics[DevelopmentalStage].evidence: No developmental stage information mentioned in manuscript or anchor data

Comment[AcquisitionMethod].evidence: data-independent acquisition (DIA) is steadily becoming more important... DIA analysis

Characteristics[CleavageAgent].evidence: digestion was carried out using trypsin (Serva, Heidelberg, Germany) at an enzyme to substrate ratio of approx. 1:27 at 37 °C overnight

Characteristics[AlkylationReagent].evidence: proteins were alkylated with iodoacetamide (13.75 mm final concentration) at ambient temperature for 30 min in the dark

Characteristics[ReductionReagent].evidence: After reduction with dithiothreitol (DTT, final concentration of 5 mm) for 20 min at 56 °C

Characteristics[Label].evidence: MS1 intensity based label-free quantification method

Characteristics[Modification].evidence: as fixed modification, only carbamidomethylation at C was set

Characteristics[Modification].1.evidence: as variable modifications oxidation (M), Gln->pyro-Glu (N-terminal Q), deamidated (NQ), ammonium (DE) and ammonia-loss (N, N-terminal C) were allowed

Characteristics[Modification].2.evidence: as variable modifications oxidation (M), Gln->pyro-Glu (N-terminal Q), deamidated (NQ), ammonium (DE) and ammonia-loss (N, N-terminal C) were allowed

Characteristics[Modification].3.evidence: as variable modifications oxidation (M), Gln->pyro-Glu (N-terminal Q), deamidated (NQ), ammonium (DE) and ammonia-loss (N, N-terminal C) were allowed

Characteristics[Modification].4.evidence: as variable modifications oxidation (M), Gln->pyro-Glu (N-terminal Q), deamidated (NQ), ammonium (DE) and ammonia-loss (N, N-terminal C) were allowed

Characteristics[Modification].5.evidence: as variable modifications oxidation (M), Gln->pyro-Glu (N-terminal Q), deamidated (NQ), ammonium (DE) and ammonia-loss (N, N-terminal C) were allowed

Characteristics[SpikedCompound].value: 13 non-mouse proteins: human α-synuclein, β-lactoglobulin from bovine milk, fibrinogen α/β/γ from human plasma, glucose oxidase from Aspergillus niger, human hemoglobin α/β, lipases 1/2/3 from Candida rugosa, lysozyme from chicken egg white, myoglobin from equine skeletal muscle
Characteristics[SpikedCompound].evidence: As spike-in proteins we chose 13 non-mouse proteins with varying physico-chemical properties: human α-synuclein, β-lactoglobulin from bovine milk, fibrinogen α, β and γ from human plasma, glucose oxidase from Aspergillus niger, human hemoglobin α and β, lipases 1, 2 and 3 from Candida rugosa, lysozyme from chicken egg white and myoglobin from equine skeletal muscle

Characteristics[SyntheticPeptide].evidence: Before LC-MS/MS analysis was performed, the iRT kit provided by Biognosys, which is required for DIA analysis using Biognosys' SN, was added according to the manufacturer's instructions

Comment[FractionationMethod].value: SDS-PAGE and High pH Reversed Phase Chromatography
Comment[FractionationMethod].evidence: Protein Fractionation by SDS-PAGE and In-gel Tryptic Digestion... Peptide Fractionation by High pH Reversed Phase Chromatography

applies_to.file_pattern: applies to library generation samples only, not to the 8 files in dataset

Comment[NumberOfFractions].evidence: each lane was cut into 10 slices... Peptides were eluted stepwise with increasing ACN concentration in 0.1% triethylamine into 8 fractions

applies_to.file_pattern: applies to library generation samples only, not to the 8 files in dataset

Comment[Separation].value: nanoHPLC Ultimate 3000 with PepMap C18 columns
Comment[Separation].evidence: For LC separation the nanoHPLC system Ultimate 3000 (Thermo Fisher Scientific) was used with a PepMap 100 C18 (100 μm ID × 2 cm, particle size 5 μm, pore size 100 Å; Thermo Fisher Scientific) as precolumn and a PepMap C18 (75 μm × 50 cm, particle size 2 μm, pore size 100 Å; Thermo Fisher Scientific) as analytical column

Comment[Instrument].evidence: Subsequently, peptides were ionized by electrospray ionization and transferred into a Q Exactive HF mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific).

Comment[FragmentationMethod].evidence: HCD fragmentation of the Top10 abundant precursor ions was performed at 27% NCE.

Comment[AcquisitionMethod].evidence: For DDA MS runs (library generation and DDA analyses)... For DIA MS runs, the MS1 full scans were performed...

Comment[IonizationType].evidence: Subsequently, peptides were ionized by electrospray ionization and transferred into a Q Exactive HF mass spectrometer

Comment[MS2MassAnalyzer].evidence: The fragment analysis (MS2) was performed with a resolution of 30,000 at 200 m/z
Comment[MS2MassAnalyzer].note: Orbitrap analyzer inferred from Q Exactive HF instrument and resolution specifications

Comment[CollisionEnergy].value: DDA: 27% NCE; DIA: stepped collision energy (25.5%, 27 and 30% NCE)
Comment[CollisionEnergy].evidence: HCD fragmentation of the Top10 abundant precursor ions was performed at 27% NCE... Stepped collision energy was used (25.5%, 27 and 30% NCE).

Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance].evidence: The precursor tolerance was set to 5 ppm and the fragment tolerance to 20 mmu.

Comment[FragmentMassTolerance].evidence: The precursor tolerance was set to 5 ppm and the fragment tolerance to 20 mmu.

Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages].evidence: A maximum of two missed cleavages was allowed.

Comment[GradientTime].evidence: Peptides were separated by a 120 min gradient using 0.1% formic acid (FA) as buffer A and 84% ACN in 0.1% FA as buffer B.
